2604.09048.title: Watt Counts: Energy-Aware Benchmark for Sustainable LLM Inference on Heterogeneous GPU Architectures
2604.09048.paper_summary: While the large energy consumption of Large Language Models (LLMs) is recognized by the community, system operators lack guidance for energy-efficient LLM inference deployments that leverage energy trade-offs of heterogeneous hardware due to a lack of energy-aware benchmarks and data. In this work we address this gap with Watt Counts: the largest open-access dataset of energy consumption of LLMs, with over 5,000 experiments for 50 LLMs across 10 NVIDIA Graphics Processing Units (GPUs) in batch and server scenarios along with a reproducible, open-source benchmark that enables community submissions to expand this dataset. Leveraging this dataset, we conduct a system-level study of LLM inference across heterogeneous GPU architectures and show that GPU selection is crucial for energy efficiency outcomes and that optimal hardware choices vary significantly across models and deployment scenarios, demonstrating the critical importance of hardware-aware deployment in heterogeneous LLM systems. Guided by our data and insights, we show that practitioners can reduce energy consumption by up to 70% in server scenarios with negligible impact on user experience, and by up to 20% in batch scenarios.
2604.09048.paper_summary_zh: 雖然社群已認識到大型語言模型（LLMs）的大量能源消耗，但系統運營商缺乏針對能源效率的LLM推理部署的指導，這是因為缺乏能源意識的基準和數據。在這項工作中，我們通過Watt Counts來填補這一空白：這是LLMs能源消耗的最大開放訪問數據集，涵蓋了在10個NVIDIA圖形處理單元（GPUs）上的50個LLMs進行的超過5,000次實驗，包含批量和伺服器情境，以及一個可重現的開源基準，該基準使社群能夠提交數據以擴展此數據集。利用這個數據集，我們對異構GPU架構下的LLM推理進行了系統級研究，並顯示GPU選擇對能源效率結果至關重要，最佳硬體選擇在模型和部署情境之間有顯著差異，這顯示了在異構LLM系統中硬體意識部署的關鍵重要性。根據我們的數據和見解，我們顯示從業者可以在伺服器情境中將能源消耗減少多達70%，而對用戶體驗的影響微乎其微，在批量情境中則可減少多達20%。
2604.09048.author: Mauricio Fadel Argerich et.al.
2604.09048.authors: Mauricio Fadel Argerich, Jonathan Fürst, Marta Patiño-Martínez
